title: Google and Apple make it easier to switch from iOS to Android
description: During the Android Show I/O Edition 2026 keynote, Google announced a meaningful improvement to the iOS to Android migration process, as it now supports more types of data. Right now, going from iOS to Android will mean you'll lose a large portion of your data. Starting with Android 17, you will be able to transfer from an iPhone to Android much more easily. The feature expands its capabilities, and it can now transfer accessibility settings, alarms, apps (free apps with user data), calendar entries, call history, email accounts, eSIM, files like images, videos, etc., messages, notes,...

title: Xiaomi 17 Max launch confirmed, design and color options teased
description: Xiaomi has taken curtains off the latest addition to its 17 series, the Xiaomi 17 Max, which is confirmed to launch in China later this month. Alongside revealing the new model, the company has also teased its design and color options. The smartphone is also available for pre-order in China. The Xiaomi 17 Max will feature a flat 6.9-inch display with slim bezels on all four sides. The phone will also sport a four-curved middle frame and a squircle-shaped rear camera module. It will be offered in three color options, one of which features a carbon fiber-inspired finish. The official...

title: Android 17 adds creator-focused features, including Instagram enhancements
description: The Android Show I/O Edition 2026 saw Google announce the new Googlebook and Gemini Intelligence. Google also announced several creator-focused features that are coming to Android 17. With Android 17, Google is introducing a new feature called Screen Reactions for both creators and everyday users. As the name suggests, the feature lets you record the screen and your front camera feed simultaneously, making it easy to add live reactions to whatever is happening on the screen. Screen Reactions will be first available on Pixel devices later this summer, likely alongside the stable...

title: Samsung clarifies the timing for the One UI 9 beta, debut set for the upcoming Galaxy Z launch
description: Yesterday, Samsung announced that the first One UI 9 beta will start rolling out in several regions later this week, but the English-language press release was murky on the timeline. Today, the company posted just about the same press release in Korean and, buried in the footnotes, are the actual details. One UI 9 beta will arrive first in Samsung’s usual testing grounds: South Korea, the US, the UK, Germany, Poland and India. However, the new press release clarifies that Poland and India will be part of the second wave of countries and there the beta program will start on May 26 (two...

title: iQOO 15T gets a launch date, key specs revealed
description: The iQOO 15T has already surfaced in several teasers and leaks, and we now have a launch date to look forward to. The brand confirmed that the 15T is set to debut in China on May 20 at 7PM local time. Alongside the launch date, iQOO posted a teaser video for the upcoming device alongside confirmation of its chipset. The latest iQOO device is getting MediaTek’s Dimensity 9500 “Monster Edition” chipset, which is a custom version of the regular 9500 with improved frame-rate stability thanks to iQOO’s in-house Q3 gaming chip. iQOO 15T design and key specs Aside from the...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold's One UI 8.5 stable update expands to more regions
description: Samsung released the stable One UI 8.5 update for the Galaxy Z TriFold last week, but it was rolling out only in South Korea. The good news is that the rollout has now expanded to three more countries - Singapore, Taiwan, and the UAE. Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold The One UI 8.5 update rolling out in these countries has firmware version F968BXXU4BZDP and comes with some new features and a new UI. If you live in any of the countries mentioned above and haven't received One UI 8.5 on your Galaxy Z TriFold yet, you can check for the update manually by navigating to your phone's Settings >...

title: Motorola Razr Fold launched in India
description: As promised, Motorola today launched the Razr Fold in India. The Razr Fold is Motorola's first book-style folding smartphone, and it will be available in India in Pantone Blackened Blue and Pantone Lily White with two memory options - 12GB/256GB and 16GB/512GB, priced at INR149,999 ($1,565/€1,340) and INR159,999 ($1,670/€1,425), respectively. [#InlinePriceWidget,14402,1#] Motorola also launched the Razr Fold's FIFA World Cup Edition (black) in India, which comes in a single 16GB/512GB configuration with special FIFA UI customizations, FIFA Heroes game, 24 karat FIFA logo plating on the...

title: More Lenovo Legion Y70 2026 specs revealed ahead of launch
description: Lenovo recently confirmed that it is returning to the smartphone market with the launch of an updated Legion Y70 on May 19. Last week, the company teased several key specifications of the 2026 phone, and more details about the device have now surfaced. The Lenovo Legion Y70 (2026) will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 chipset and feature a 5,500mm² vapor chamber. The processor will be paired with LPDDR5X Ultra RAM and UFS 4.1 storage. On the front, the phone will sport a Q10 AMOLED LTPO display with a 144Hz refresh rate and 2K resolution. The panel will also support a 360Hz...

title: Samsung is now blocking apps that spam your notifications with ads
description: Samsung's Device Care app has recently received an update to version 13.8.80.7. This one is notable because it brings with it a new feature that could prove to be very useful to many. The app can now detect and block apps that appear to be spamming your notification area with ads. The apps that exhibit this behavior will be put in deep sleep to prevent them from bothering you again. Basic blocking uses a list of "apps identified by Samsung as sending frequent ads" and blocks these when they're detected on your phone. The intelligent blocking option constantly analyses your...

title: Oppo Find X9 Ultra and Find X9s are coming to India next week
description: After weeks of teasers, Oppo has finally confirmed the India launch date for its camera-focused flagship, the Find X9 Ultra. The phone was originally unveiled in China on April 21. Alongside, Oppo is also bringing the Find X9s to India. The Oppo Find X9 Ultra and the Find X9s will debut in India on May 21, the company confirmed via social media. As per a recent leak, the Ultra could retail for around INR 1,69,999, whereas the Find X9s could start at INR 70,000 for the base variant. Notably, one of the Ultra's main competitors, the vivo X300 Ultra, was recently launched in India at a...

title: Apple won't be able to get F1 rights for the UK and Italy for at least six years
description: Starting with the 2026 season, the broadcast rights holder for Formula 1 in the US is Apple, which is offering the competition through its Apple TV+ streaming service over there. Apple hasn't officially declared that it wants to do a similar deal internationally, but it stands to reason that it might have at least looked at some of the biggest markets. Unfortunately, three of those are now out of consideration since Sky has just renewed its agreements for broadcasting F1 in the UK, Ireland, and Italy. The contract has been renewed through 2032 in Italy and through 2034 in the UK and...

title: Another report confirms July 22 for Samsung's next Unpacked launch event
description: Samsung's Galaxy Glasses will be unveiled this July alongside the Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8, a new report from Korea now claims. The grand Unpacked event where all of these products will be introduced is said to be taking place in London on July 22. The Galaxy Watch9 series will also be made official on the same stage. The much-rumored Galaxy Fold Wide is also likely to be there, since Samsung may want to pre-empt the arrival of the iPhone Ultra in September with a very similar form factor. The Galaxy Glasses are designed in partnership with Gentle Monster, and are running...

title: Dimensity 9600 leak suggests major single-core gains and class-leading GPU performance
description: The next wave of flagship Android phones and tablets will, for the most part, use chipsets from either Qualcomm or MediaTek. We’ve already seen some details for the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6, and we are now getting our first bits of info for the Dimensity 9600. Tipster Digital Chat Station revealed that the upcoming flagship SoC from MediaTek will be fabricated on a 2nm process node, without confirming whether it will be TSMC’s N2P or Samsung’s SF2. Either way, the chip is expected to utilize a 2+3+3 all-big core CPU architecture with a focus on delivering top-notch single-core...

title: Sony Xperia 1 VIII review
Sony Xperia 1 VIII unveiled with larger 48MP telephoto sensor, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5
description: The new Sony Xperia 1 VIII has a new 1/1.56” sensor inside its telephoto camera – it’s four times larger than the sensor inside the Mark 7 model and it has four times the resolution too. The new model also redesigns the camera island, jumps to the latest Qualcomm chipset all while still maintaining trademark Xperia features like the expandable storage, the 3.5mm headphone jack and the two-stage shutter key. Let’s go back to the cameras – Sony changed course in a major way. The Mark 7, 6, 5 and 4 had continuous optical zoom like on a point-and-shoot camera. Even the Mark 3 could switch...

title: eBay rejects GameStop's takeover offer
description: eBay has rejected GameStop's offer to buy it for $56 billion, calling it "neither credible nor attractive", Bloomberg is reporting. The offer came last week, when GameStop said it would pay $125 per eBay share. Intriguingly, GameStop's market value is about $11 billion, while eBay's is $45 billion, so this is definitely a case of a small fish wanting to eat a much bigger one. The GameStop bid is half cash and half GameStop stock, and represents a 20% premium over eBay's current stock price. GameStop will have to borrow $20 billion to finance the acquisition, but when asked its CEO couldn't...

title: Deal: the Samsung Galaxy S25+ is 30% off right now on Amazon US
description: In our head-to-head comparison article, the Samsung Galaxy S25+ and Galaxy S26+ traded blows – well, “blows” may be too strong a word considering how similarly the two performed. And as we noted in that article, the older 2025 Plus is cheaper. Right now, Amazon US has a 256GB Galaxy S25+ at $700 – that’s $300 less than when the phone launched. Meanwhile, the S26+ got a price hike and the 256GB model normally costs $1,100, though that is discounted to a more reasonable $925 at the moment. Samsung Galaxy S25+ ...

title: T-Mobile MVNO lets an AI clone your voice, handle some of the calls for you
description: A Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) on T-Mobile's network, called REALLY, announced a new AI-based pilot project. It aims to build an AI assistant trained on your voice to handle trivial calls for you. The company's CEO says the AI clone is not meant to replace you, but to help focus on what's important. The feature is pretty straightforward. The AI trains on your voice, speaking style and communication preferences. Your AI clone will then pick up calls you don't want to handle, like reservation confirmations, sales agents, customer support, etc. Once the call is over, the AI will...

title: Verizon's new bundle gives you home internet and a mobile line for $69.99 per month
description: Verizon is introducing a new affordable bundle today. You pay $69.99 per month and get both Verizon mobile and Fios home internet. That Fios connection is a 300Mbps one for $19.99, and for mobile you're getting on the Unlimited Welcome plan for $50. Caveats? Sure, let's go through them. "Plus taxes and fees", first and foremost - that's how you should read the prices above. Then, for the mobile plan, you need to bring your own phone and set up Auto Pay for both that and the home internet plan. Oh, and you need to be a new customer. And there's a $99 setup fee. And after three years, you'll...

title: Google introduces Googlebooks running Android
description: Move over, Chromebooks, Googlebooks are coming. That's right - Googlebooks. These aren't just laptops, you see - they are "designed for Gemini Intelligence", which sounds like Google desperately wanted to have a name that sounds close enough to Apple Intelligence, but maybe we're reading too much into it. Anyway, Googlebooks will help Google move "from an operating system to an intelligence system", and they feature "the best of Android" and Chrome OS. This sounds like it is, in effect, the much-anticipated Aluminum OS, even though Google has not once called it that. There will be...
